guides.text: We've met $\bot$ (bottom, falsity) — the always-false formula. From $\bot$, anything follows via [ex-falso|ex falso]. Now meet its dual: $\top$ (top, truth). $\top$ is **always provable** from any context. While $\bot$ lets you conclude anything, $\top$ can be concluded from anything. The engine parses `T` as $\top$.
The command `top-intro` is an axiom — it takes zero arguments. Truth needs no evidence.

Look at line 1: $\vdash \top$. Empty context, conclusion $\top$. The simplest possible proof — truth stands on its own, no assumptions needed. Paired with $\bot$ (problem 19), this gives us the two endpoints of logic: utter falsity and absolute truth.

unlocks.name: top-intro
unlocks.description: From anything, truth follows. ⊤ is always provable — an axiom requiring no premises. The dual of ex-falso.
